Shrine|Q131456780|WD=Q131456780|1=Tomi Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Tomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tomi-jinja Kamisha.jpg|260px]]
Tomi Shrine Kamitsuo-sha |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}Sakurai 1176 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|23.3|N|135|51|14.3|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tomi Shrine}} |祭神 =Kamitsuo-sha:
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}
Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}):
{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}):
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/CCP.html|title=Ten Shrines of Tomi|accessdate=2018-09-07}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} 4th year, spring, 2nd month |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |別名 = Notomiya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=190|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=190|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is identified with the '''Tomi Shrine''' of Shikinokami District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was called '''Notomiya'''. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition this shrine was originally located on {{ill|lt=Torimiyama|Q18700112|WD=Q18700112|1=Mount Torimi}}. Torimiyama is said to be the sacred ritual ground (matsuriniwa) where {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Japan's first {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, worshipped the imperial ancestral deities and heavenly deities in spring, second month, of his fourth regnal year after enthronement''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', volume 3.. The Nihon Shoki account that he first worshipped the imperial ancestral deities after enthronement is also considered the origin and first stage of the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai#History|Q11433653|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijōe|1=Daijosai}}. The deity of Kamitsuo-sha, which corresponds to the main shrine, is given as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, though there is also a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was listed as a shikinaisha, but in {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1112|Q19525|WD=Q19525|1=1112}}), a landslide caused the shrine buildings to be moved to the present location. In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it was promoted from village shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the 1950s, {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was founded in the precincts. There are 13 stone monuments in the precincts (Man'yō poem monuments, haiku monuments, and waka monuments). A distinctive feature of this shrine is that its {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} is depicted in human form. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - Kamitsuo-sha: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} * Enshrined deities - Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTomi Shrine (Q11602724)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTomi Shrine (Q11602724)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is identified with the '''Tomi Shrine''' of Shikinokami District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was called '''Notomiya'''. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition this shrine was originally located on {{ill|lt=Torimiyama|Q18700112|WD=Q18700112|1=Mount Torimi}}. Torimiyama is said to be the sacred ritual ground (matsuriniwa) where {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Japan's first {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, worshipped the imperial ancestral deities and heavenly deities in spring, second month, of his fourth regnal year after enthronement''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', volume 3.. The Nihon Shoki account that he first worshipped the imperial ancestral deities after enthronement is also considered the origin and first stage of the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai#History|Q11433653|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijōe|1=Daijosai}}. The deity of Kamitsuo-sha, which corresponds to the main shrine, is given as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, though there is also a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was listed as a shikinaisha, but in {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1112|Q19525|WD=Q19525|1=1112}}), a landslide caused the shrine buildings to be moved to the present location. In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it was promoted from village shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the 1950s, {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was founded in the precincts. There are 13 stone monuments in the precincts (Man'yō poem monuments, haiku monuments, and waka monuments). A distinctive feature of this shrine is that its {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} is depicted in human form. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - Kamitsuo-sha: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} * Enshrined deities - Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QTomi Shrine (Q11602724)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QTomi Shrine (Q11602724)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)}}[[Category:qq]]

Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]] [[Category:qq]]

Shrine|Q131456780|WD=Q131456780|1=Tomi Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Tomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tomi-jinja Kamisha.jpg|260px]]
Tomi Shrine Kamitsuo-sha |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}Sakurai 1176 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|23.3|N|135|51|14.3|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tomi Shrine}} |祭神 =Kamitsuo-sha:
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}
Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}):
{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}):
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/CCP.html|title=Ten Shrines of Tomi|accessdate=2018-09-07}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} 4th year, spring, 2nd month |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |別名 = Notomiya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=190|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=190|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is identified with the '''Tomi Shrine''' of Shikinokami District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was called '''Notomiya'''. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition this shrine was originally located on {{ill|lt=Torimiyama|Q18700112|WD=Q18700112|1=Mount Torimi}}. Torimiyama is said to be the sacred ritual ground (matsuriniwa) where {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Japan's first {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, worshipped the imperial ancestral deities and heavenly deities in spring, second month, of his fourth regnal year after enthronement''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', volume 3.. The Nihon Shoki account that he first worshipped the imperial ancestral deities after enthronement is also considered the origin and first stage of the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai#History|Q11433653|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijōe|1=Daijosai}}. The deity of Kamitsuo-sha, which corresponds to the main shrine, is given as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, though there is also a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was listed as a shikinaisha, but in {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1112|Q19525|WD=Q19525|1=1112}}), a landslide caused the shrine buildings to be moved to the present location. In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it was promoted from village shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the 1950s, {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was founded in the precincts. There are 13 stone monuments in the precincts (Man'yō poem monuments, haiku monuments, and waka monuments). A distinctive feature of this shrine is that its {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} is depicted in human form. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - Kamitsuo-sha: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} * Enshrined deities - Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]]

Shrine|Q131456780|WD=Q131456780|1=Tomi Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Tomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tomi-jinja Kamisha.jpg|260px]]
Tomi Shrine Kamitsuo-sha |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}Sakurai 1176 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|23.3|N|135|51|14.3|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tomi Shrine}} |祭神 =Kamitsuo-sha:
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}
Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}):
{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}):
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/CCP.html|title=Ten Shrines of Tomi|accessdate=2018-09-07}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} 4th year, spring, 2nd month |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |別名 = Notomiya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=190|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=190|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is identified with the '''Tomi Shrine''' of Shikinokami District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was called '''Notomiya'''. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition this shrine was originally located on {{ill|lt=Torimiyama|Q18700112|WD=Q18700112|1=Mount Torimi}}. Torimiyama is said to be the sacred ritual ground (matsuriniwa) where {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Japan's first {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, worshipped the imperial ancestral deities and heavenly deities in spring, second month, of his fourth regnal year after enthronement''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', volume 3.. The Nihon Shoki account that he first worshipped the imperial ancestral deities after enthronement is also considered the origin and first stage of the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai#History|Q11433653|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijōe|1=Daijosai}}. The deity of Kamitsuo-sha, which corresponds to the main shrine, is given as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, though there is also a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was listed as a shikinaisha, but in {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1112|Q19525|WD=Q19525|1=1112}}), a landslide caused the shrine buildings to be moved to the present location. In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it was promoted from village shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the 1950s, {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was founded in the precincts. There are 13 stone monuments in the precincts (Man'yō poem monuments, haiku monuments, and waka monuments). A distinctive feature of this shrine is that its {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} is depicted in human form. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - Kamitsuo-sha: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} * Enshrined deities - Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良県の旧県社]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]]

category|Category:Tomi-jinja (Sakurai, Nara)}} [[JA:等彌神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11602724}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Another shrine in Nara Prefecture, Nara City: {{ill|lt=Tomi Shrine|Q131456780|WD=Q131456780|1=Tomi Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Tomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tomi-jinja Kamisha.jpg|260px]]
Tomi Shrine Kamitsuo-sha |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}Sakurai 1176 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|23.3|N|135|51|14.3|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tomi Shrine}} |祭神 =Kamitsuo-sha:
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}
Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}):
{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}):
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/CCP.html|title=Ten Shrines of Tomi|accessdate=2018-09-07}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} 4th year, spring, 2nd month |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |別名 = Notomiya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=190|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=190|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is identified with the '''Tomi Shrine''' of Shikinokami District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was called '''Notomiya'''. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition this shrine was originally located on {{ill|lt=Torimiyama|Q18700112|WD=Q18700112|1=Mount Torimi}}. Torimiyama is said to be the sacred ritual ground (matsuriniwa) where {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Japan's first {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, worshipped the imperial ancestral deities and heavenly deities in spring, second month, of his fourth regnal year after enthronement''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', volume 3.. The Nihon Shoki account that he first worshipped the imperial ancestral deities after enthronement is also considered the origin and first stage of the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai#History|Q11433653|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijōe|1=Daijosai}}. The deity of Kamitsuo-sha, which corresponds to the main shrine, is given as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, though there is also a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was listed as a shikinaisha, but in {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1112|Q19525|WD=Q19525|1=1112}}), a landslide caused the shrine buildings to be moved to the present location. In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it was promoted from village shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the 1950s, {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was founded in the precincts. There are 13 stone monuments in the precincts (Man'yō poem monuments, haiku monuments, and waka monuments). A distinctive feature of this shrine is that its {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} is depicted in human form. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - Kamitsuo-sha: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} * Enshrined deities - Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]]

category|Category:Tomi-jinja (Sakurai, Nara)}} [[JA:等彌神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11602724}} == Japanese content == {{混同|Another shrine in Nara Prefecture, Nara City: {{ill|lt=Tomi Shrine|Q131456780|WD=Q131456780|1=Tomi Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Tomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tomi-jinja Kamisha.jpg|260px]]
Tomi Shrine Kamitsuo-sha |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}Sakurai 1176 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|23.3|N|135|51|14.3|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tomi Shrine}} |祭神 =Kamitsuo-sha:
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}
Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}):
{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}):
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/CCP.html|title=Ten Shrines of Tomi|accessdate=2018-09-07}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} 4th year, spring, 2nd month |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |別名 = Notomiya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=190|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=190|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is identified with the '''Tomi Shrine''' of Shikinokami District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was called '''Notomiya'''. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition this shrine was originally located on {{ill|lt=Torimiyama|Q18700112|WD=Q18700112|1=Mount Torimi}}. Torimiyama is said to be the sacred ritual ground (matsuriniwa) where {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Japan's first {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, worshipped the imperial ancestral deities and heavenly deities in spring, second month, of his fourth regnal year after enthronement''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', volume 3.. The Nihon Shoki account that he first worshipped the imperial ancestral deities after enthronement is also considered the origin and first stage of the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai#History|Q11433653|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijōe|1=Daijosai}}. The deity of Kamitsuo-sha, which corresponds to the main shrine, is given as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, though there is also a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was listed as a shikinaisha, but in {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1112|Q19525|WD=Q19525|1=1112}}), a landslide caused the shrine buildings to be moved to the present location. In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it was promoted from village shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the 1950s, {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was founded in the precincts. There are 13 stone monuments in the precincts (Man'yō poem monuments, haiku monuments, and waka monuments). A distinctive feature of this shrine is that its {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} is depicted in human form. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - Kamitsuo-sha: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} * Enshrined deities - Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:桜井市の建築物]]

Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was called '''Notomiya'''. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition this shrine was originally located on {{ill|lt=Torimiyama|Q18700112|WD=Q18700112|1=Mount Torimi}}. Torimiyama is said to be the sacred ritual ground (matsuriniwa) where {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Japan's first {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, worshipped the imperial ancestral deities and heavenly deities in spring, second month, of his fourth regnal year after enthronement''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', volume 3.. The Nihon Shoki account that he first worshipped the imperial ancestral deities after enthronement is also considered the origin and first stage of the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai#History|Q11433653|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijōe|1=Daijosai}}. The deity of Kamitsuo-sha, which corresponds to the main shrine, is given as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, though there is also a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was listed as a shikinaisha, but in {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1112|Q19525|WD=Q19525|1=1112}}), a landslide caused the shrine buildings to be moved to the present location. In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it was promoted from village shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the 1950s, {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was founded in the precincts. There are 13 stone monuments in the precincts (Man'yō poem monuments, haiku monuments, and waka monuments). A distinctive feature of this shrine is that its {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} is depicted in human form. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - Kamitsuo-sha: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} * Enshrined deities - Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakurai]]

Shrine|Q131456780|WD=Q131456780|1=Tomi Shrine}}}} {{神社 |名称 = Tomi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Tomi-jinja Kamisha.jpg|260px]]
Tomi Shrine Kamitsuo-sha |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}Sakurai 1176 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|30|23.3|N|135|51|14.3|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tomi Shrine}} |祭神 =Kamitsuo-sha:
{{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}
Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}):
{{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}):
{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/CCP.html|title=Ten Shrines of Tomi|accessdate=2018-09-07}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system#other shrines|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}} 4th year, spring, 2nd month |本殿 = One-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} cypress-bark roof |別名 = Notomiya |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 5|Q2550|WD=Q2550|1=May 5}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Tomi Shrine|position=bottom|width=190|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=190|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tomi Shrine''' (とみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small), and its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is identified with the '''Tomi Shrine''' of Shikinokami District, {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|WD=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. Until the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period it was called '''Notomiya'''. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but according to shrine tradition this shrine was originally located on {{ill|lt=Torimiyama|Q18700112|WD=Q18700112|1=Mount Torimi}}. Torimiyama is said to be the sacred ritual ground (matsuriniwa) where {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, Japan's first {{ill|lt=emperor|Q208233|WD=Q208233|1=Emperor of Japan}}, worshipped the imperial ancestral deities and heavenly deities in spring, second month, of his fourth regnal year after enthronement''{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|WD=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', volume 3.. The Nihon Shoki account that he first worshipped the imperial ancestral deities after enthronement is also considered the origin and first stage of the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai#History|Q11433653|WD=Q11433653|lt=Daijōe|1=Daijosai}}. The deity of Kamitsuo-sha, which corresponds to the main shrine, is given as {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, though there is also a theory that it is {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=Nigihayahi}}. In the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} it was listed as a shikinaisha, but in {{ill|lt=Ten'ei|Q1193915|WD=Q1193915|1=Ten'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1112|Q19525|WD=Q19525|1=1112}}), a landslide caused the shrine buildings to be moved to the present location. In {{ill|lt=1940|Q18633|WD=Q18633|1=1940}} ({{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|WD=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 15), it was promoted from village shrine to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. In the 1950s, {{ill|lt=Sakurai|Q127026|WD=Q127026|1=Sakurai, Nara}} {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} was founded in the precincts. There are 13 stone monuments in the precincts (Man'yō poem monuments, haiku monuments, and waka monuments). A distinctive feature of this shrine is that its {{ill|lt=Yatagarasu|Q745463|WD=Q745463|1=Yatagarasu}} is depicted in human form. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deity - Kamitsuo-sha: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} * Enshrined deities - Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|WD=Q261637|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=Hachiman}}): {{ill|lt=Emperor Jinmu|Q200188|WD=Q200188|lt=Iware Myōjin|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ojin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} / Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|lt=Kasuga deity|Q11088805|WD=Q11088805|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=Kasuga-no-Kami}}): {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi-no-Kami|1=Takamimusubi}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|WD=Q2564508}} === Precincts === * Kamitsuo-sha * Haiden - newly built in {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|WD=Q2173|1=1924}} ({{ill|lt=Taishō|Q329174|WD=Q329174|1=Taishō era}} 13). * Shinsensho * Sanshūjo * Yumihari-sha - deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakurai Yumihari Imperial Princess|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Inari deity|Q719665|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} shrine * Kokuryū-sha * Shimotsuo-sha left hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kasuga-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) * Shimotsuo-sha right hall ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}) ** Shimotsuo-sha haiden * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ebisu|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Atago Shrine|Q713021|WD=Q713021|lt=Atago-sha|1=Atago Shrine (Kyoto)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konpira|1=UNKNOWN}} shrine * {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|WD=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} Daijinja * Momo Shrine - deity: {{ill|lt=Ōkamuzumi|Q11073596|WD=Q11073596|lt=Ōkamuzumi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkamuzumi no Mikoto}} * Shrine office * Sakurai City {{ill|lt=Gokoku Shrine|Q1534477|WD=Q1534477|1=Gokoku Shrines}} ファイル:Tomijinja石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii ファイル:Tomijinja参道.jpg|Approach ファイル:Tomijinja下津尾社.jpg|Shimotsuo-sha ファイル:Tomijinja上津尾社.jpg|Kamitsuo-sha === Access === * About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=JR West|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} / {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Railway|Q1531085|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|lt=Sakurai Station (Nara)|Q5361714|WD=Q5361714|lt=Sakurai Station|1=Sakurai Station (Nara)}} * From JR/Kintetsu Sakurai Station, bus for {{ill|lt=Tanzan Shrine|Q488618|WD=Q488618|1=Tanzan Shrine}}, get off at "Kaminomori-chō," then 2 minutes on foot === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://www.city.sakurai.lg.jp/kanko/syaji/kashiramoji/tagyo/1395297487203.html Tomi Shrine (Tomi Jinja)] (Sakurai City) * [http://www3.pref.nara.jp/miryoku/megurunara/inori/syaji/area03/tomijinjya/ Tomi Shrine] (Meguru Nara) * {{Wayback|url=http://www7b.biglobe.ne.jp/~yn118/ |title=Tomi Shrine |date=20151112024259}} (official website archive) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tomi Shrine (Q11602724)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:桜井市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakurai]]
